The prophecy concerning Egypt's destruction is very detailed. Those who side with God’s enemies will be with them in punishment. The king of Babylon and his army will be instruments of this destruction. God often makes one wicked person a scourge to another. No place in the land of Egypt will escape the fury of the Chaldeans. The Lord is known by the judgments he executes. Yet these are only present effects of divine displeasure, not worthy of our fear, compared with the wrath that is to come, from which Jesus delivers his people.

Egypt will grow weaker and weaker. If lesser judgments do not succeed in humbling and reforming sinners, God will send greater ones. God justly breaks that power which is abused, either to inflict wrongs upon people or to deceive them. Babylon will grow stronger. It is futile for people to try to bind up the arm the Lord is pleased to break, and to strengthen those whom he will bring down. Those who disregard the revelations of his truth and mercy will know his power and justice in the punishment for their sins.
